analysis —
law a covenant is the standing [[law]] entered into — a two-way bond with [[balance]] pledged on both sides, [[seal]]ed and content-addressed ([[identity]]) so it cannot be forged or forgotten and inherits down the [[dna]] lineage ([[merge]]); distinct from one-way [[grace]] and from the un-entered rule, restored after breach by turning back ([[return]]).
